model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the Sign In button
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (361,205) said dx=220 dy=361 conf=1.00 -> (581,566)
  iter 1: at (581,566) said dx=-81 dy=-116 conf=0.80 -> (500,450)
  iter 2: at (540,508) said dx=0 dy=-70 conf=0.90 -> (540,438)
  iter 3: at (540,490) said dx=80 dy=-30 conf=0.90 -> (620,460)
  iter 4: at (560,483) said dx=58 dy=-10 conf=0.90 -> (618,473)
  iter 5: at (575,480) said dx=47 dy=-10 conf=0.90 -> (622,470)
  iter 6: at (587,478) said dx=40 dy=-5 conf=0.90 -> (627,473)
  iter 7: at (597,477) said dx=30 dy=0 conf=0.90 -> (627,477)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to click the "Sign In" button.
The red crosshair is currently located on the "Sign In" button, but it's slightly to the left of the center.
Looking at the image, the red crosshair is at approximately (477, 656) based on the image coordinates. Wait, the provided coordinates in the prompt history are different. Let's look at the image.
The crosshair is centered horizontally on the blue button, but a bit to the left of the "Sign In" text.
Actually, the "Sign In" button spans from roughly x=353 to x=647 and y=623 to y=683.
Center of button: x = (353+647)/2 = 500, y = (623+683)/2 = 653.
The crosshair is at roughly (477, 656) according to the provided coordinates in the bottom right? No, (604, 476) is just a reference.
Wait, the red crosshair in the image is at approximately (477, 656) in the 1000x1000 normalized coordinate system? No, the image is 1000x500? Let's check.
The image resolution is 1000x500.
The crosshair is at (477, 656) if the image was 1000x1000.
Actually, let's look at the visual. The crosshair is very close to the center of the blue button.
The button is from x=353 to x=647. The center is 500.
The crosshair is at x=477. Offset dx = 500 - 477 = 23.
The button is from y=623 to y=683 (normalized to 1000). Wait, the image height is 500.
Actually, let's just use the visual.
The crosshair is slightly to the left of the center of the "Sign In" button.
The "Sign In" text is to the right of the crosshair.
The center of the button is roughly halfway between the left edge and the right edge of the blue area.
Crosshair is at x=477. Button center is at x=500. dx = 23.
Crosshair is at y=656. Button center is at y=653. dy = -3.
Let's re-estimate.
The crosshair is almost exactly in the center vertically.
Horizontally, it's a bit to the left.
The distance from crosshair to center seems to be about 20-30 pixels.
Actually, looking closely at the image, the crosshair is quite close to the center.
Let's say dx=23, dy=0, confidence=0.9.
